steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1 states that for several centuries Italian was the default language for opera scores. Sentence 2 states that opera singers today still encounter a wide range of Italian terms when reading those scores. The second sentence is a direct result of the first—because Italian dominated opera for centuries, modern singers still meet Italian terminology. The blank needs a transition that signals consequence.

steps.title: Test each transition word against that relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals contrast—the second sentence would push back against the first. Singers encountering Italian terms is a result of Italian’s dominance, not a contradiction of it. ✗
  • Choice B — Thus,: signals that what follows is a logical result of what came before, fitting the cause-and-effect relationship exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — Lastly,: signals the final item in an enumerated list. No list has been set up in the preceding sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Granted,: signals a concession before a counterargument. No counterargument appears in the second sentence. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
